title: marsdenia tinctoria
intro: Marsdenia tinctoria, also known as the "sacred musk vine" or "Indian ipecacuanha," is a plant species native to India, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Marsdenia tinctoria include its ability to relieve coughs, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by boiling the roots in water, and the resulting extract can be consumed as a tea to harness its medicinal properties.
